productFP.statement: Provide custom cross-sectional metal shapes with tight dimensional tolerances and selectable surface hardness — in near-net-shape geometry that eliminates 40-80% of downstream machining waste
productFP.context: with tight dimensional tolerances and selectable surface hardness
differentiators: Near-net-shape geometry eliminates 40-80% material removal vs. machining from bar stock
IT8 tolerance class (+-0.02 mm) achievable via cold finishing per ISO 286
Selective surface hardening up to 64 HRC at controllable depth 0.5-5 mm, ductile core retained
Full alloy library: carbon, alloy, bearing, spring, and tool steels through common forming equipment
In-line induction hardening — no outsourcing to third-party heat treaters, shorter lead time
IATF 16949 certified for direct automotive OEM supply

commodityFP.statement: Provide elongated metal shapes with defined cross-sectional geometry for structural, mechanical, or guiding functions
commodityFP.reasoning: All steel profile products — standard I-beams, custom precision profiles, hot-rolled sections, cold-drawn bars — share the fundamental function of providing shaped steel for integration into assemblies. Precision, geometry complexity, and surface properties differentiate suppliers; the commodity-level promise is vendor-agnostic. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]
commodityFP.fpExtension: The underlying precision cross-section forming and controllable surface hardening capability also addresses: (1) precision linear motion systems requiring hardened running surfaces (guide rails, slideways), (2) tribological interface profiles for sliding/bearing contacts, (3) custom mechanical retention profiles (keyway inserts, spline sections in elongated form). Secondary Phase A search: industries requiring precision-formed wear-resistant guide profiles for linear motion or sliding-contact interfaces. [ASM: derived from feature set in 01_product_decomposition]

bomPosition.level: L1-L2 — Semi-finished material / near-net-shape input component [SRC]
bomPosition.position: Upstream material supplier providing shaped steel consumed by OEM manufacturers, Tier 1 suppliers, and specialist fabricators
bomPosition.grandparentSystem: Customer end product (machinery, vehicle, elevator, firearm, medical device, industrial equipment)

complements.name: Customer geometry specification (drawing or CAD)
complements.description: Defines die geometry; no custom profile can be produced without a defined cross-section

complements.name: Downstream cutting / facing operation
complements.description: Profiles delivered as bars or coils; customer must cut to final part length

complements.name: Secondary machining capability (drill, mill, grind)
complements.description: Most assembly applications require holes, chamfers, or mating features not producible via rolling

complements.name: Quality inspection equipment (CMM, hardness tester)
complements.description: Verifying IT8 tolerance compliance and surface hardness requires calibrated measurement

complements.name: Surface coating or treatment
complements.description: Corrosion protection, paint, or plating for environmental-exposure applications

complements.name: PPAP documentation system
complements.description: Required for automotive OEM supply under IATF 16949; not needed for non-automotive customers
